candidates.headline: WeForge opens its doors
candidates.summary: The platform repository's initial commit and the tracker's launch announcement establish the launch of WeForge as a self-hosted forge.

source_trail.excerpt_or_detail: Initial commit 'feat: bootstrap weforge platform' introducing the service scaffolding.
Launch announcement: 'WeForge is live for project hosting.'

candidates.headline: The tracker becomes the front door
candidates.summary: Within the launch window, service requests move into the meta tracker; the ops journal implies this became the required intake path, though no policy record states it.

source_trail.excerpt_or_detail: First service request filed as a tracker issue rather than out-of-band.
'Asking everyone to file requests on the tracker from now on.'

candidates.headline: The May outage recovery left no record
candidates.summary: The record shows an outage was reported and that service resumed, but nothing records how. The recovery procedure exists only in operator memory.

source_trail.excerpt_or_detail: Outage reported May 12; issue closed May 14 with no resolution comment. No postmortem was looked for and found — none is linked from the issue.
No journal entry exists between May 12 and May 27; the recovery window is silent.

candidates.headline: CI minutes triple in week four
candidates.summary: Repository activity suggests rapid CI growth at the end of the window; quantifying it needs corpus the brief did not point at.

source_trail.excerpt_or_detail: Workflow configuration churn across week four consistent with CI load growth.
